title: Cure with Facts — Digital Citizenship and Misinformation Resilience Program
note: Delivered sessions on platform misinformation and source verification as part of the Digital Citizenship and Misinformation Resilience Program, helping undergraduate students examine how false or misleading content travels online and how to verify claims more responsibly.

title: OSINT fundamentals for newsrooms
note: Six-part modular workshop covering reverse image search, geolocation, whois/DNS reconnaissance, social-media provenance, archive.today workflows, and source-chain documentation. 300+ participants trained to date.

title: Verification for broadcast producers
note: Hands-on session on verifying viral video, identifying synthetic media, and writing corrections without losing the audience.

title: Data journalism & public-data investigations
note: Module taught within the BSS Media Studies programme — covers scraping, spreadsheet investigations, and ethical data visualisation.

title: Algorithmic accountability for public-sector communicators
audience: Oxford Saïd / UNESCO cohort — alumni meetup
note: Post-programme peer session on translating the Oxford Saïd executive curriculum into South Asian governance contexts.

title: Rumour cascades during political transitions
note: Case study on the July 2024 protests, using open-source tools to trace rumour spread across Telegram and Facebook.

title: Academic English for journalism students
note: Ongoing teaching module on academic writing, fossilized-error correction, and argumentative structure for undergraduates in journalism tracks.
